name: onboarding_longhaul_resume_no_reask
notes: The onboarding analog of the manager long-haul retention test: owner gives name+type EARLY, then a MULTI-turn digression (a capability question + chitchat) intervenes, then the owner returns and the manager must RESUME collection asking ONLY the still-missing field — NOT re-asking name or type. Distinct from onboarding_question_mid_collection_then_resume (a SINGLE mid-collection question); this is a multi-turn digression under load. Core no-re-ask objective in onboarding. Setup: `setup --owner-inputs true --journey --name convo-harness-<id>` then `script <tenant> onboarding_longhaul_resume_no_reask.json`.

steps.message: It's Verma Kirana, a general provisions store.
steps.note: Owner gives NAME (Verma Kirana) + TYPE (general store) early.

steps.message: Wait — before that, can you also handle my Instagram, or is it just WhatsApp?
steps.note: Digression T3: capability question mid-collection. Manager answers honestly; must retain the name/type from T2.

steps.message: Achha. Anyway Diwali rush is starting, crazy busy these days.
steps.note: Digression T4: chitchat. Manager holds context across a non-collection turn.

steps.message: Ok, where were we? Let's finish this.
steps.note: Manager RESUMES collection after the 2-turn digression, asking only the still-missing field (city/about) — MUST NOT re-ask the name or type given in T2. Re-asking a fact stated 3 turns ago under digression load = the exact live-failure regime.
